improve:
Look at the current state of the project — run status.sh, review recent logs, understand the failure patterns and bottlenecks. Then implement the highest-impact improvement you can find. This could be: better prompts that help the model understand problems, faster execution paths, fewer LLM calls, stronger oracle verification, new capability unlocks. Whatever moves the needle most. Be practical: implement it, test it, verify no regressions. Commit your work.

⚠️  NEVER delete scripts/, prompts.json, package.json scripts, or CLAUDE.md — these are infrastructure other agents depend on.
research:
Study the codebase deeply. Read through the core modules (src/llm/task-agent.ts, src/domains/auto-detect.ts, src/agents/, src/main.ts). Trace the hot path from problem → classify → oracle → baseline → repair → task-agent → supervisor. Identify the weakest link — the thing that, if improved, would unlock the most progress. Look at failure logs to understand what's actually failing and why. Write your analysis as a clear document with concrete, actionable proposals. What would make the biggest difference and why?

⚠️  This is a research task — read and analyze, don't delete or restructure.
frontier:
Find the hardest unsolved problems — look at the very-hard and hard tiers. Run them, study why they fail. Don't just patch symptoms — understand the capability gap. What fundamental thing can't the model do? Is it cryptographic precision? Multi-step planning? Numerical stability? Once you identify the gap, figure out what architectural or prompt changes could close it. Then implement the most promising approach and test it. The goal is to expand what the engine CAN solve, not just make existing solutions faster.

⚠️  Work on a branch. Don't delete infrastructure — other agents are working here too.
review:
Review the codebase honestly. Look at recent commits, benchmark results, failure patterns. Are we actually improving or just adding complexity? Is anything getting worse? Are there components that should be removed because they don't earn their keep? Are there patterns that keep failing that we're not addressing? Write a candid assessment. If something isn't working, say so. If we're optimizing the wrong thing, say so. Cut what needs cutting, but never infrastructure that other agents depend on.

⚠️  Be critical of PIPELINE CODE (src/). Be PROTECTIVE of infrastructure (scripts/, prompts.json, package.json scripts, CLAUDE.md).
step-back:
Zoom out. Don't look at specific failures or code — think about the project as a whole. What's the biggest lever we haven't pulled yet? What assumptions are we making that might be wrong? What would a 10x better version of this engine look like, and what's the first concrete step toward it? Are we solving the right problems? Is our architecture sound? Is our core bet (tools + execution feedback + oracle verification > single LLM call) paying off? Think strategically, then identify one concrete, high-impact action to take next.

⚠️  This is a thinking task. Write your analysis. Don't delete anything.
tidy:
Simplify the codebase carefully. Look for: dead code that's never called, over-engineered abstractions that could be simpler, deep call chains that could be flattened, duplicated logic that could be shared. Remove what's truly dead. Simplify what's overly complex. But prioritize safety: run typecheck and tests after every change. If you're unsure whether something is used, leave it.

🚫  DO NOT DELETE: scripts/ (any file), prompts.json, package.json scripts, CLAUDE.md, stream-filter.ts, or any shell script. These are infrastructure. Other agents depend on them.

✅  FOCUS ON: src/ directory — pipeline code, agents, LLM modules. That's where simplification matters.
harden:
Make the engine's verification more robust. Look at oracles that produce false positives (pass when they should fail) or false negatives (fail when they should pass). Look at edge cases that aren't covered. Look at oracle hardening — are stubs being properly rejected? Are numerical tolerances appropriate? Improve the oracles, add edge cases, make verification stricter. Every oracle that catches a bad solution is a win. Every oracle that passes a wrong answer is a bug.

⚠️  Work on test/oracle quality, not on deleting things. Run the benchmark after changes to confirm no regressions.
accelerate:
Find the slowest part of the development loop and make it faster. This could be: reducing LLM calls for simple problems (are we over-engineering the pipeline?), improving cache hit rates (are cache keys too narrow?), speeding up execution (are sandboxes slow to start?), better prompt caching (are we getting cache misses?), or streamlining the diagnostic flow (do agents spend too many turns debugging?). Profile first, then fix the biggest bottleneck. Measure before and after.

⚠️  Optimize the ENGINE, not the infrastructure. Don't remove scripts or tools — make the pipeline faster.
explore:
Explore the codebase with fresh eyes. Read through modules you haven't seen before. Look for interesting patterns, hidden bugs, untapped opportunities. You're not fixing a specific problem — you're discovering what needs attention. Follow your curiosity. When you find something worth improving, improve it. When you find a bug, fix it. When you find an opportunity, pursue it. The goal is open-ended discovery that leads to concrete improvements.

⚠️  Be curious, not destructive. Read before you write. Understand before you change. Never delete infrastructure files.
dx:
Make the development experience seamless — for both humans and agents. Look at the full cycle: status → pick problem → run → read logs → diagnose → fix → re-test → benchmark. Where is the friction? Are logs hard to parse quickly? Are failure modes unclear? Do scripts require too many manual steps? Is feedback too slow? Improve: script output formatting, error messages, caching behavior, diagnostic automation, log readability. Every second saved in the debug loop compounds across hundreds of runs.

⚠️  Improve scripts/ output and tooling. Don't delete scripts — make them better.
